label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking which of the following sentences is NOT true based on the text about Bobby Moresco. Let me go through each option one by one. Option A: "His father did not support his work as a bartender." Wait, the text mentions that when Moresco was in Hollywood, he worked as a waiter and drove a cab. His father told him to stop this craziness and get a job because he had a wife and daughter. So, the father was against him working as a waiter and cab driver, right? So, if A says "work as a bartender", but in the text it's "waiter". Hmm. The job is different. The text says "waiter", not "bartender". So maybe A is not true because it's about being a bartender, but the actual job was a waiter. So A is incorrect. Option B: "Before he became an overnight success, his life experienced ups and downs." The text shows he struggled in the 70s, moved to Hollywood, had a wife and daughter, faced financial difficulties, and then his brother was killed, leading to writing, then moving back, then another period of unemployment in 2003. So yes, his life had ups and downs before the success with Crash. So B is true. Option C: "His brother's death inspired his writing Half-Deserted Streets." The text says after his brother's murder in 1983, he moved back to his old neighborhood and started writing as a way to explore the pain of Hell's Kitchen. The play was based on his brother's killing. So C is true. Option D: "Moresco grew up in New York's Hell's Kitchen which is a few blocks east of Broadway." The first sentence says Hell's Kitchen lies right next door to Broadway. So if Hell's Kitchen is right next to Broadway, then being a few blocks east is plausible. The text says he
